steps.title: Spot the mutual influence
steps.content: The phrase “in turn” signals a reciprocal cycle—ocean forces damage structures, whose repairs then alter those same forces.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean bidirectional influence — waves damage structures, and repairs to structures alter wave patterns in return. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Interplay: "exemplify the interplay between marine structures and ocean forces" — interplay means the way two things interact and influence each other reciprocally, matching the back-and-forth described. ✓
  • Choice B — Tension: "exemplify the tension between marine structures and ocean forces" — tension means opposition or strain; the passage describes a two-way influence, not merely conflict. ✗
  • Choice C — Incongruity: "exemplify the incongruity between marine structures and ocean forces" — incongruity means a lack of harmony or mismatch; the passage describes interaction, not mismatch. ✗
  • Choice D — Continuum: "exemplify the continuum between marine structures and ocean forces" — continuum means a continuous sequence; this doesn't capture the mutual causation between two distinct agents. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
